response.justification: Bogdan is a strong candidate with a unique and highly relevant background. His experience founding and developing GISCollective, a geo-data platform, directly aligns with the geospatial nature of the role. While his professional C++ experience is not recent, he has extensive and deep expertise in Dlang, a systems programming language with similarities to C++, which he has used to build low-latency, high-performance microservices. This, combined with his experience in distributed systems and his demonstrated passion for the geospatial domain, makes him a compelling applicant worth interviewing, despite not meeting the primary language requirement directly.
strengths: Deep geospatial experience from founding and building GISCollective, a geo-data platform.
Extensive systems programming experience with Dlang in low-latency, high-performance environments, a strong proxy for C++/Rust.
Proven initiative and versatility, with a history of founding projects and working across the full stack from embedded to web.
concerns: Lack of recent, extensive commercial experience in modern C++ or Rust, which is a primary requirement.
No explicit mention of experience with routing algorithms or map tile optimization, which are core to the role.
Recent commercial roles have focused on a TypeScript/Node.js stack, which is different from the core technologies of this position.
